framework.title: Revenue Leak Diagnostic Engine Scoring Framework
framework.description: A governed scoring framework that converts questionnaire responses into dimension scores, leak signals, integrity bands, and intervention priority without relying on unsupported financial forecasts or black-box inference.
framework.methodology_note: All outputs in v1 are derived from declared questionnaire inputs, governed weights, reverse-scoring rules, and deterministic aggregation logic. No predictive claims, market comparisons, or hidden model behavior are used.

missing_answer_policy.reason: v1 does not permit structural diagnostics from incomplete declared conditions.

dimensions.title: Trust Integrity
dimensions.description: Measures the extent to which the path sustains confidence, legitimacy, reassurance, and decision-stage trust.
dimensions.interpretation_note: Lower scores indicate that confidence architecture is weaker than the commitment being requested.

dimensions.title: Flow Continuity
dimensions.description: Measures continuity of movement from entry through action without interruption, disorientation, or transition decay.
dimensions.interpretation_note: Lower scores indicate structural interruption or movement failure across the path.

dimensions.title: Decision Clarity
dimensions.description: Measures how clearly the offer, value, commitment, and next step are expressed at decision-relevant moments.
dimensions.interpretation_note: Lower scores indicate that users are being asked to decide without enough interpretive support.

dimensions.title: Friction Containment
dimensions.description: Measures how effectively the path avoids avoidable effort, complexity, and burden during progression.
dimensions.interpretation_note: Lower scores indicate that the path is introducing more burden than the user should need to absorb.

dimensions.title: Recovery Readiness
dimensions.description: Measures the system's ability to restore intent after hesitation, interruption, or abandonment.
dimensions.interpretation_note: Lower scores indicate that interrupted intent is likely being abandoned instead of reclaimed.

total_integrity_score.title: Total Funnel Integrity Score
total_integrity_score.description: The governed aggregate score representing the structural integrity of the path across the five primary dimensions.
total_integrity_score.methodology_note: v1 uses equal weighting across the five structural dimensions to preserve interpretability and avoid artificial prioritization at the aggregate layer.

integrity_bands.title: Structurally Sound
integrity_bands.interpretation: The path appears structurally stable across the primary integrity dimensions, though local refinement may still be valuable.

integrity_bands.title: Minor Leakage
integrity_bands.interpretation: The path remains fundamentally viable but exhibits meaningful leakage in one or more dimensions.

integrity_bands.title: Moderate Weakness
integrity_bands.interpretation: The path shows significant structural weakness that is likely suppressing conversion performance.

integrity_bands.title: Serious Leakage
integrity_bands.interpretation: The path contains major structural failures that materially degrade continuity, clarity, trust, or recovery.

integrity_bands.title: Critical Integrity Failure
integrity_bands.interpretation: The path is structurally compromised to the point that meaningful conversion integrity cannot be assumed.

selection_logic.primary_leak_class_rule: The lowest normalized leak-class signal score becomes the primary leak class when it is materially distinct.
selection_logic.secondary_leak_class_rule: The second-lowest normalized leak-class signal score becomes the secondary leak class when it remains diagnostically meaningful.
selection_logic.tie_handling_rule: If the difference between the two lowest class scores is less than or equal to 3 points, dual-state interpretation is permitted.
selection_logic.upstream_override_rule: If Message-Path Misalignment is severe and its score is within 5 points of the lowest class, it may take strategic priority because it contaminates downstream interpretation.

leak_classes.title: Trust Collapse
leak_classes.methodology_note: Lower scores indicate that commitment is being requested without sufficient confidence support.

leak_classes.title: Flow Break
leak_classes.methodology_note: Lower scores indicate that intent is not being carried through the path with adequate continuity.

leak_classes.title: Friction Overload
leak_classes.methodology_note: Lower scores indicate that burden and procedural heaviness are structurally suppressing movement.

leak_classes.title: Decision Ambiguity
leak_classes.methodology_note: Lower scores indicate that users are under-supported in understanding the offer and acting on it.

leak_classes.title: Recovery Absence
leak_classes.methodology_note: Lower scores indicate that lost intent is not being structurally recovered.

leak_classes.title: Message-Path Misalignment
leak_classes.methodology_note: Lower scores indicate that the path is not maintaining continuity between entry promise and action logic.

rules.reason: Alignment failure corrupts interpretation before downstream remediation can be trusted.
Trust deficits often block commitment even when other dimensions are partially functional.
If the user cannot interpret the decision clearly, downstream interventions lose leverage.
Flow failure disrupts movement itself and can neutralize otherwise viable demand.
Friction reduction is critical but should be ordered in relation to continuity and clarity.
Recovery systems become more valuable once the core path is at least minimally viable.

secondary_priority_logic.selection_rule: The secondary leak class may produce a secondary plug class when its signal score remains below the secondary meaningfulness threshold and is not redundant with the primary class.
secondary_priority_logic.redundancy_rule: If the secondary plug class is structurally identical to the primary recommendation, collapse into a single recommendation.
